component.name: Scriptura
component.summary: GNOME-native Bible study with SWORD modules and Strong's lexicon

description.p: Scriptura is a focused, native Linux Bible study application that pairs a quiet two-pane reading layout with the depth of SWORD Project modules.

ul.li: Two independent panes — read two translations side by side, with per-pane sync locks.
Strong's lexicon — click any Hebrew or Greek word for the original lexeme, morphology, and a word study across the book.
Per-verse annotations — four highlight colors, underlines, notes with topical tags; all in-place edits with no scroll jumps.
Cross-references — OpenBible.info's 340,000-reference database with a TSK fallback.
Full-text search — Whoosh-backed per-module index with a canon distribution chart and case-sensitive option.
Study Journal — master-detail browser for every annotation across every module, with tag management.
Reading plans — six built-in plans with day-by-day progress tracking.
Devotionals — Spurgeon's Morning and Evening and others with multi-section detection.
Generic Books — Didache, Westminster Confession, Apostolic Fathers, and other tree-keyed reference works with a table-of-contents browser.
eBible.org translations — modern licensed Bibles (LEB, BSB, etc.) not packaged for SWORD.

description.p: All data is stored locally. No telemetry. Network access only for downloading modules and reference data that you explicitly request.

screenshot.caption: Two-pane reading with the Strong's lexicon open on a Greek word
Two translations side by side, sharing a verse selection
Study Journal — every annotation in one filterable surface
Built-in reading plans with day-by-day progress
F11 reading mode hides all chrome for distraction-free reading

description.p: First Flathub release. Renamed from Bible Reader to Scriptura.

ul.li: Pane-swap button preserves reading position across the swap via per-module memory.
bible: URI scheme — external apps can deep-link to a reference.
Current-verse indicator marks the active verse subtly without looking like a selection.
Atomic writes across all state files protect annotations from crash-mid-save corruption.
LRU caps on chapter and Strong's caches keep memory bounded during long sessions.
Lazy Whoosh import and cheap module-presence probe shave startup time.
Pane search subsystem extracted into its own module for clean separation.

description.p: First public testing release. Includes everything in the milestone build (M1–M8) plus the post-release expansion: adjustable reading-column width, master-detail Study Journal with tag management, module picker with language filter and per-module info, F11 reading-mode exit affordance, F3 / Shift+F3 search step-through with case toggle and match highlighting, Generic Books reader with TOC navigation, and XDG-compliant data paths.
